# Coding Standards

## Purpose

This document defines engineering standards for the CTOS Portfolio repository.

Applies to:

- Human Developers
- Claude Code
- Codex
- Cursor Agent
- Roo Code
- Windsurf

---

# Engineering Philosophy

Build for:

- Maintainability
- Readability
- Scalability
- Accessibility
- Performance

Avoid building for short-term convenience.

---

# Source Of Truth

Priority Order:

1. PRD Documents
2. Roadmap Layers
3. AI Agent Operating System
4. Coding Standards
5. Existing Code

---

# Architecture Rules

## Rule CS-01

Astro First.

Use Astro whenever hydration is not required.

---

## Rule CS-02

Vue Islands Only.

Vue is reserved for interactive experiences.

---

## Rule CS-03

Static First.

Prefer build-time generation.

---

## Rule CS-04

Server Rendering Only When Necessary.

---

# File Structure Rules

Every file must have a clear ownership.

Example:

src/
├── components/
├── features/
├── layouts/
├── pages/
├── content/
├── lib/
└── types/

---

# Naming Conventions

## Components

PascalCase

Examples:

HeroSection.astro
ProjectCard.vue
CertificateVault.vue

---

## Composables

camelCase with use prefix

Examples:

useSearch.ts
useTheme.ts
useMotion.ts

---

## Utilities

camelCase

Examples:

formatDate.ts
calculateReadingTime.ts

---

## Constants

UPPER_SNAKE_CASE

Examples:

MAX_SEARCH_RESULTS
DEFAULT_LANGUAGE

---

# TypeScript Rules

## Required

Strict Mode

Explicit Types

Shared Interfaces

---

## Forbidden

any

ts-ignore

Untyped API Responses

---

# Component Standards

Every component must:

- Have a single responsibility
- Be reusable
- Be accessible
- Be documented

---

# Props Rules

Required:

- Explicit typing
- Default values where appropriate

Forbidden:

- Unbounded props
- Implicit contracts

---

# Styling Standards

Primary:

Tailwind CSS

---

Avoid:

Inline styles

Magic numbers

Duplicated utilities

---

# Design Token Rules

Colors must come from tokens.

Spacing must come from scale.

Typography must come from system definitions.

---

# Accessibility Standards

Required:

Keyboard Navigation

Focus States

ARIA Labels

Reduced Motion Support

Semantic HTML

---

# Motion Standards

Motion must:

- Support meaning
- Guide attention
- Improve understanding

---

Motion must not:

- Distract
- Reduce performance

---

# Performance Standards

Targets:

Lighthouse >= 95

Accessibility >= 100

SEO >= 100

Best Practices >= 100

---

# Content Standards

Content must be:

- Concise
- Evidence-based
- Professional

Avoid:

Buzzwords

Marketing fluff

Exaggerated claims

---

# Git Standards

Commit Format:

feat(scope): description
fix(scope): description
docs(scope): description
refactor(scope): description

---

Examples:

feat(hero): add architecture graph

docs(roadmap): update motion layer

---

# Pull Request Standards

Every PR must include:

- Objective
- Scope
- Impact
- Validation

---

# Review Checklist

Before Merge:

- Build passes
- Types pass
- Accessibility validated
- Performance validated
- No dead code
- No duplicated logic

---

# Anti-Patterns

Forbidden:

- Massive components
- God objects
- Deep prop drilling
- Duplicate utilities
- Unused dependencies
- Premature abstractions

---

# AI Agent Rules

Rule CS-AI-01

Read roadmap before implementation.

Rule CS-AI-02

Optimize for maintainability.

Rule CS-AI-03

Prefer simple solutions.

Rule CS-AI-04

Document architectural decisions.

Rule CS-AI-05

Reject changes that reduce UX, accessibility, or performance.

---

# Definition Of Compliance

A contribution is compliant when:

- It follows roadmap requirements.
- It follows architecture rules.
- It passes quality validation.
- It improves the repository.
- It does not introduce technical debt.
